page_content: INAG206 Agricultural Business Law (3 Credits) This course introduces various legal concepts and their relationships to agricultural business and transactions. Topics include torts, criminal law, contracts, promissory notes, property, partnerships, business entities, employment, and bankruptcy, among others. Students will gain a general understanding of the legal system that will help them manage and/or operate proﬁtable agricultural businesses. Restriction: Students at the Institute of Applied Agriculture (IAA); or students at the College of Agriculture and Natural Resources (AGNR); or permission may be granted to other students based on available space. Additional Information: Course cannot be used to fulﬁll a requirement for a Robert H. Smith School of Business major or minor.
